sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: Position size = f * (-Trade P&L / Biggest Historical Loss). Optimal f value requires separate optimization (not specified in excerpt). Uses geometric mean of trade multipliers to determine portfolio growth. Formula: TWR = ∏[1+f*(-Trade/Biggest Loss)]^(1/N); Growth = (TWR)^(1/N)

source_rule_text: Position size is determined by multiplying the fraction of capital (f) by the ratio of each trade's loss to the biggest historical loss, with the geometric mean of all trade multipliers determining overall portfolio growth.
